suggested_titles.title: Veridian Grounding: Abstract Forms
suggested_titles.subtitle: A study in elemental balance and striking contrast.
suggested_titles.description: An abstract composition featuring bold red forms anchored on a deep green field, with a dark, foundational element below.

suggested_titles.subtitle: Textured layers of color creating spatial and emotional depth.
suggested_titles.description: Dynamic red and dark blue elements against a verdant background, suggesting a profound landscape or architectural abstraction.

suggested_titles.title: Elemental Resonance: A Modern Study
suggested_titles.subtitle: Exploring color, tension, and form in a contemporary abstract expression.
suggested_titles.description: Vibrant and intense color blocks within a thoughtful arrangement, evoking both stillness and energetic contrast.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork in a highly contemporary, refined, and undistracting setting, emphasizing its form and color in a museum-like yet accessible manner. It underscores the artwork's artistic value and adaptability to modern, sophisticated spaces.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Contemporary art gallery space with restrained architecture.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Sleek and contemplative, characterized by architectural clarity and a focus on the artwork. Cool, serene, and modern, with an art-first sensibility, enhanced by the dramatic low perspective.
materials: Matte mineral white walls with subtle texture
Polished light grey concrete floor
Simple white ceiling
Discreet track lighting fixtures
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, diffused gallery daylight from a high, unseen source, complemented by subtle, directional track lighting creating gentle shadows that highlight the artwork's texture and volumetric presence.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Nadir Controlado: Camera is positioned very low, near the floor, approximately 20-35 cm from the ground, using a controlled nadir-inspired architectural viewpoint. The floor plane, wall height, ceiling lines, columns, door frames or architectural verticals may rise strongly in the composition. Use a controlled oblique angle across the room, not a straight-on product view, with one coherent vanishing direction for architecture and artwork. Use the nadir effect mainly through the surrounding room geometry: floor lines, vertical wall planes, ceiling height, architectural edges, columns, furniture legs or door frames. The artwork remains the visual subject, but the dramatic low viewpoint is carried by the architecture rather than by warping the canvas.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the low camera to establish deep vanishing lines and reveal foreground floor texture, placing the artwork as a commanding focal point within the receding space.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The extremely low camera angle and oblique view dramatically emphasize the height and presence of the artwork and the surrounding architectural lines, creating a dynamic, almost monumental perspective. It highlights the physical depth of the gallery and the artwork's commanding position, making the canvas feel significant and grounded while maintaining optical fidelity.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's bold abstract forms and intense color palette find a powerful stage in this minimalist gallery. The clean, neutral environment allows its visual tension and emotional depth to resonate without distraction. The 'Nadir Controlado' camera perspective aligns with the artwork's grounding elements (the lower black/blue block), giving it a commanding, almost sculptural presence within the architectural space.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context positions the artwork as museum-worthy and highly collectible, appealing to contemporary art buyers and collectors. The minimalist setting demonstrates its versatility for modern interiors, while the dramatic camera angle enhances its premium, investment-grade appeal, making it feel substantial and architecturally integrated, conveying sophistication and artistic merit.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A pristine contemporary art gallery space, clean and minimalist. The artwork (exact reference image) is centrally mounted on a matte mineral white wall with subtle texture, appearing elevated from a low vantage point. The floor is polished light grey concrete, extending prominently into the foreground, showing subtle texture and reflections, with distinct vanishing lines. The ceiling is simple white, receding upwards into the distance. Subtle, directional gallery track lighting illuminates the artwork from above, creating soft, defined shadows that emphasize its texture and physical depth. Architectural lines of the room, including the floor edge and wall planes, converge dynamically towards a single vanishing point, emphasizing deep spatial coherence. A very low, oblique camera view, positioned approximately 25cm from the floor, capturing the artwork from below and slightly to the side, showcasing its physical presence and the rising, clean architectural elements. No visible people, no animals.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no winter default unless seasonally intended, no monumental atrium, no heroic scale, no architectural spectacle, no busy gallery, no crowd, no art fair signage, no pet, no animal, no generic neutral interior, no showroom, no unrelated gallery
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Nadir Controlado: Camera is positioned very low, near the floor, approximately 20-35 cm from the ground, using a controlled nadir-inspired architectural viewpoint. The floor plane, wall height, ceiling lines, columns, door frames or architectural verticals may rise strongly in the composition. Use a controlled oblique angle across the room, not a straight-on product view, with one coherent vanishing direction for architecture and artwork. Use the nadir effect mainly through the surrounding room geometry: floor lines, vertical wall planes, ceiling height, architectural edges, columns, furniture legs or door frames. The artwork remains the visual subject, but the dramatic low viewpoint is carried by the architecture rather than by warping the canvas.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork as a focal point in a refined, contemplative domestic collection, highlighting its artistic presence and material quality within an intimately scaled, culturally literate environment.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Intimate, serene, and contemplative. A cultivated domestic atmosphere with quiet sophistication, allowing the artwork to command attention as the primary cultural element. Soft, filtered morning light enhances the pensive mood, evoking a sense of calm and focused introspection.
materials: Walls in warm, subtly textured limewash in a muted ivory or soft taupe
Polished dark wood floor
Thick, hand-knotted wool rug in a muted, earthy tone
Bespoke, low-slung, dark wood console table with a minimalist design (partially visible)
Understated bronze floor lamp with a linen shade
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, diffused natural light from a large, unseen window, creating gentle shadows and highlights. The illumination emphasizes the artwork's texture and color depth without glare, evoking a calm spring morning or late afternoon.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Contrapicado 7/8: Camera height is low, below normal knee height, with the viewpoint grounded close to the floor but still architectural. Use a 7/8 oblique rotation so the wall, artwork depth, and room volume are visible through one shared perspective. The artwork is seen with strong side depth and believable thickness; use clear asymmetry and negative space toward the vanishing side while keeping surrounding wall and floor aligned to the same camera.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the artwork to be central and imposing from the low vantage point, while revealing the refined spatial context.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The low camera angle elevates the artwork, making it feel monumental and central to the space. The 7/8 oblique view provides strong depth perception of the canvas's physical presence and the room's elegant proportions, grounding the artwork within a sophisticated architectural narrative. Prominent floor and rising architectural lines enhance the perspective.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's bold, minimalist forms and contemplative color palette (deep green with vibrant red and subtle blue/black accents) resonate with the quiet sophistication of a private collector's space. Its singular presence becomes the meditative anchor of the room, inviting focused engagement. The muted, warm backdrop of the limewash wall allows the artwork's colors and textures to stand out prominently, reinforcing its abstract landscape or architectural suggestion.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context elevates the artwork's perceived value by associating it with a discerning, cultivated lifestyle and an intimate, curated collection. It demonstrates the artwork's ability to function as a significant cultural statement piece in a premium domestic setting, appealing to collectors seeking art with depth, quiet authority, and a modern sensibility. The intentional camera angle enhances its visual gravitas and physical presence, suggesting a carefully considered acquisition.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A quiet collector's living room, intimate and culturally literate. The artwork, a vibrant abstract canvas dominated by green with bold red and black geometric forms, is wall-hung and centered on a warm, subtly textured limewash wall in a muted ivory or soft taupe. The room features a polished dark wood floor, visible in the foreground and midground, partially covered by a thick, hand-knotted wool rug in an earthy taupe. A bespoke, low-slung, dark wood console table with a minimalist design is positioned in the foreground, partially cropped by the camera's low vantage point. A single, understated bronze floor lamp with a cylindrical linen shade stands to one side, adding a subtle vertical architectural element that rises with the camera's view. Soft, diffused natural light from a large unseen window illuminates the scene, creating gentle shadows and enhancing the artwork's texture and volumetric presence. Artwork in its original proportions, colors, and textures, perfectly installed and prominent. No visible people, no animals, no branding.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no winter default unless seasonally intended, no hotel catalog, no furniture showroom, no unrelated gallery, no cluttered space.
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Contrapicado 7/8: Camera height is low, below normal knee height, with the viewpoint grounded close to the floor but still architectural. Use a 7/8 oblique rotation so the wall, artwork depth, and room volume are visible through one shared perspective. The artwork is seen with strong side depth and believable thickness; use clear asymmetry and negative space toward the vanishing side while keeping surrounding wall and floor aligned to the same camera.

contextual_proposals.context_name: The Serene Alcove of Reflected Light
contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork as a focal point within a sophisticated, quietly luxurious domestic environment, emphasizing its contemplative depth and rich colors under natural, warm light, suggesting it belongs to a discerning collector.
contextual_proposals.space_type: A refined living area or quiet lounge within an aspirational private residence.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Tranquil, contemplative, warm, and sophisticated, bathed in the soft, golden glow of late afternoon light filtering through a large window. An atmosphere of refined calm and understated luxury.
materials: Limewash walls in a warm neutral tone (subtle cream or very pale taupe)
Wide-plank pale oak flooring
Clean, plaster ceiling
Premium, contemporary armchair in natural linen upholstery (muted olive tone)
Low-profile, dark wood (walnut or dark oak) console table
Subtle bronze or aged brass details (e.g., in a minimalist lamp base if applicable, but not required)
contextual_proposals.lighting: Polished daylight from a large window, diffused and warm, mimicking a late afternoon golden hour. The light gently illuminates the wall where the artwork hangs, creating soft shadows and highlighting the artwork's texture without glare. Indirect ambient lighting further enhances the refined mood.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Reflejo Dorado de Tarde / Palazzo: Camera height is elegant human standing height, approximately 150-170 cm, as if observed calmly from inside a palazzo room. Use a soft three-quarter rotation that catches reflected late light while keeping the artwork plane legible. The composition is stable, warm, and architectural, with golden afternoon reflection supporting the room rather than repainting the artwork; keep elegant balance but avoid perfect symmetry unless the room itself naturally demands it.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Medium-close, allowing the artwork to dominate while revealing enough of the refined architectural context and the play of light.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The camera captures the artwork from an elegant human standing height, with a soft three-quarter left-oblique rotation (about 10-15 degrees) that reveals the physical thickness of the canvas and the depth of the room. The composition is stable and architectural, with vanishing lines leading subtly into the room's depth. The golden afternoon light is reflected gently, highlighting the artwork's materiality and integrating it seamlessly into the warm, premium domestic setting.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's deep greens and vibrant reds find a complementary yet refined home in a space characterized by warm neutrals and natural light. The strong, abstract forms resonate with the clean lines and noble materials of quiet luxury. The contemplative atmosphere of the room enhances the artwork's inherent silence and dramatic tension, allowing its colors and composition to breathe and become a focal point for reflection.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context positions the artwork as a high-value piece for discerning collectors who appreciate understated elegance and meaningful art integration within their homes. The aspirational domestic setting, bathed in flattering natural light, makes the artwork feel inviting, accessible yet premium, and suitable for sophisticated living spaces. It shows the artwork's versatility to enrich a refined personal environment.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A refined living room in an aspirational private residence, bathed in soft, golden late afternoon window light. The walls are warm neutral limewash, the floor is wide-plank pale oak. On one wall, centered on a spacious, well-lit section, hangs the full artwork, appearing as a physical, real stretched canvas with visible texture and thickness. The room features subtle architectural details and a quiet luxury aesthetic. A premium, contemporary armchair upholstered in natural linen, in a muted olive tone, is partially visible in the mid-ground, positioned slightly to the right, adding depth without distracting. A low-profile, dark wood console table with minimalist lines is beneath the artwork, partially visible to establish scale. The camera is at elegant human standing height, looking at the artwork with a soft three-quarter left-oblique rotation, capturing reflected late light and architectural depth. The composition is stable, warm, and architectural, showing coherent vanishing lines and a clear foreground, midground, and background. Minimal depth of field ensures artwork and immediate surroundings are crisp. No people, no animals.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no winter default, no cold light, no harsh shadows, no excessive furniture, no bright influencer colors, no hotel catalog, no furniture showroom.
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Reflejo Dorado de Tarde / Palazzo: Camera height is elegant human standing height, approximately 150-170 cm, as if observed calmly from inside a palazzo room. Use a soft three-quarter rotation that catches reflected late light while keeping the artwork plane legible. The composition is stable, warm, and architectural, with golden afternoon reflection supporting the room rather than repainting the artwork; keep elegant balance but avoid perfect symmetry unless the room itself naturally demands it.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork within a historically rich, yet visually refined European architectural setting, highlighting its contemporary abstract power against a backdrop of enduring elegance and warm, natural light.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Refined historical, serene, warm, contemplative, with a golden late afternoon ambiance.
materials: Aged limewash plaster walls in a muted cream
Large-format pale honed Travertine stone floor
Tall arched windows with slender dark metal frames
Minimal dark wood console table
contextual_proposals.lighting: Golden late day light streaming through tall arched windows, casting long, soft shadows and illuminating the artwork with a warm glow, creating a gentle chiaroscuro effect.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Vista Aérea con Contexto de Ventanas: Camera height is elevated above normal eye level, high enough to read the room, windows, floor plane, and artwork together. Use a gentle diagonal rotation so window context and the artwork wall are both visible without splitting perspectives. The room is comprehensible from above, with window light and floor geometry giving context while the artwork remains identifiable; allow a moderate offset so floor, windows, and surrounding architecture carry part of the frame.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The elevated, slightly oblique view allows the artwork's scale to be understood in relation to the grand architectural proportions and the expansive floor plane, while emphasizing the room's depth and the quality of natural light. The slight rotation prevents a flat rendering and includes valuable contextual elements like windows and floor patterns, enhancing its physical presence.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's strong, minimalist abstract forms and bold color palette (deep green, vibrant red) are elegantly counterpointed by the historical warmth and restrained grandeur of a European palazzo. The aged materials and warm late light soften the artwork's starkness, allowing its forms to resonate with the architectural lines without being overwhelmed. The tension between contemporary abstraction and historical setting enriches both.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context elevates the artwork to a premium, collectible status, suggesting it belongs in sophisticated, historically aware collections. The warm, inviting light and architectural context appeal to buyers seeking art that enhances established, refined spaces, demonstrating its versatility beyond modern minimalist settings. The aerial view offers a unique, aspirational perspective on how the art integrates into a significant interior.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: Elevated aerial view, Vista Aérea con Contexto de Ventanas, of a grand European palazzo room, bathed in golden late day light. The artwork, a deep olive green canvas with bold red and dark blue abstract forms, is hung on an aged limewash plaster wall, off-center to reveal architectural context. The room features tall arched windows with slender dark metal frames, allowing warm, soft light to flood the expansive honed Travertine stone floor. The floor has a subtle pattern visible from above. A minimal dark wood console table is positioned on the floor plane below the artwork, contributing to the room's geometry. The camera looks down and across the room, capturing the floor plane, the artwork wall, and the window context in a single coherent composition. Little to no depth of field, all architectural elements and the artwork are crisp. No people, no animals.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no winter default, no period drama, no museum spectacle, no rustic farmhouse.
contextual_proposals.camera_first_slot_name: Vista Aérea con Contexto de Ventanas
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Vista Aérea con Contexto de Ventanas: Camera height is elevated above normal eye level, high enough to read the room, windows, floor plane, and artwork together. Use a gentle diagonal rotation so window context and the artwork wall are both visible without splitting perspectives. The room is comprehensible from above, with window light and floor geometry giving context while the artwork remains identifiable; allow a moderate offset so floor, windows, and surrounding architecture carry part of the frame.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork in an authentic, refined working environment, emphasizing its material presence and artistic context without distracting clutter.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Quiet, contemplative, professional, bathed in soft daylight, with subtle hints of creative process and premium restraint.
materials: Smooth painted plaster wall (light, neutral tone)
Light concrete floor with subtle texture
Subtle faint pin marks on the wall (only in periphery)
Minimalist studio ceiling with integrated soft lighting tracks
Possibly a stacked, bare canvas or wooden easel in the receding background
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, diffused north light entering from an unseen large window or skylight, creating gentle, even illumination with subtle highlights on the artwork's texture. No harsh shadows.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Pasillo, Obra Descentrada y Próxima: Camera height is natural standing or slightly lowered human height, close enough to feel the corridor edge. Use an offset three-quarter corridor angle, with the artwork deliberately off-center and close rather than symmetrically framed. Place the artwork on a long wall plane rather than a short frontal display wall. The wall should extend laterally into the scene like a gallery passage, corridor, or elongated architectural plane, creating a clear vanishing direction. Use floor lines, ceiling lines, wall joints, lighting tracks, windows, columns, doorways, or distant wall planes to reinforce depth. The artwork may sit off-center and relatively close to the viewer, while the architecture recedes naturally to one side. Avoid product-style centered symmetry.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Medium-close to controlled architectural distance, allowing the artwork to feel substantial while revealing the receding depth of the studio space.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The oblique three-quarter angle reveals the physical thickness and dimensionality of the canvas, making it feel like a real object in a tangible space. The off-center placement and receding architecture create dynamic spatial tension, drawing the eye along the wall and into the studio depth while keeping the artwork dominant.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: Placing the artwork within a clean, premium artist's studio grounds it in its creative origins. The neutral, understated environment allows the artwork's bold colors and abstract forms to stand out, highlighting its artistic integrity and professional execution.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context lends authenticity and gravitas to the artwork, appealing to buyers who value the artistic process and a refined, yet functional, aesthetic. It suggests the artwork is ready for a discerning collector's space, having originated from a place of considered creation. The clean atelier enhances its premium market positioning.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A clean artist's working studio with premium restraint. The large abstract painting, featuring a dominant red rectangle on a green background, hangs off-center on a long, smooth painted plaster wall. The wall extends laterally into the scene like a gallery passage, with a light concrete floor showing subtle texture. Soft, even north light fills the space, casting gentle shadows. Subtle traces of artistic process are visible in the receding background, such as a bare stacked canvas or faint pin marks on a distant wall, but the main wall around the artwork is pristine. The camera is at natural standing height, capturing an offset three-quarter corridor angle, with the architecture receding naturally to one side, emphasizing depth through visible floor lines and the extended wall plane. The artwork and its immediate surroundings are in sharp focus, with a natural photographic depth-of-field falloff in the distant parts of the studio. No people, no animals.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no messy clutter, no wet paint chaos, no generic neutral interior, no unrelated gallery, no luxury corridor, no showroom.
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Pasillo, Obra Descentrada y Próxima: Camera height is natural standing or slightly lowered human height, close enough to feel the corridor edge. Use an offset three-quarter corridor angle, with the artwork deliberately off-center and close rather than symmetrically framed. Place the artwork on a long wall plane rather than a short frontal display wall. The wall should extend laterally into the scene like a gallery passage, corridor, or elongated architectural plane, creating a clear vanishing direction. Use floor lines, ceiling lines, wall joints, lighting tracks, windows, columns, doorways, or distant wall planes to reinforce depth. The artwork may sit off-center and relatively close to the viewer, while the architecture recedes naturally to one side. Avoid product-style centered symmetry.

contextual_proposals.context_role: Presents the artwork as a centerpiece in a sophisticated, architecturally aware collector's space, highlighting its bold simplicity and material depth within a midcentury-modern context.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Refined, architectural, contemplative, with a subtle midcentury warmth. A sense of spaciousness and curated living with an understated 'burgundy-toned' spatial mood.
materials: Polished concrete walls and floor
Warm off-white plaster wall
Dark walnut wood panel accent
Aged dark brown leather (on a collector chair)
Dark bronze details
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, filtered warm daylight entering from large, industrial-style windows, casting gentle shadows and highlighting the texture of the concrete. Subtle ambient warmth, with a refined period floor lamp providing additional soft glow, contributing to the 'burgundy-toned' spatial mood.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Borgoña Recovecos / Cámara 3/4 en Loft de Hormigón: Camera height is human but tucked into an architectural recess, approximately 120-160 cm depending on the loft edge. Use a 3/4 view from a recess, corner, or architectural edge, with a burgundy-toned spatial mood and one clean vanishing direction. The artwork is partially contextualized by loft recesses and concrete architecture, creating depth and intimacy without hiding the piece; allow strong off-center framing through recesses, corners, side planes, or architectural edges.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The 3/4 view from a concrete architectural recess captures the artwork at a dynamic angle, revealing its physical depth and integrating it into the sophisticated loft architecture. The strong vanishing lines of the concrete structure will draw the eye towards the artwork while establishing the room's elegant proportions. The off-center framing emphasizes the artwork's bold composition and allows the textural interplay with the concrete surroundings to be appreciated. The camera's perspective enhances the artwork's presence as a three-dimensional object within a curated space.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's strong, minimalist forms and bold color palette resonate with the clean lines and material honesty of midcentury design. The green and red contrast beautifully with the neutral, textural backdrop of concrete and warm wood, allowing the artwork to command attention. The abstract 'horizon' line in the artwork finds an echo in the architectural planes and spatial depth of the loft, creating a harmonious dialogue between art and environment.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context elevates the artwork by placing it in a premium, architecturally significant environment that appeals to collectors of modern and midcentury design. It showcases the artwork's versatility for sophisticated, contemporary spaces, suggesting it is a statement piece for discerning buyers. The subtle integration of a 'burgundy-toned' mood, hinted at by the camera, adds a layer of curated elegance and visual interest, making the presentation highly aspirational.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: An abstract painting with a large red rectangle on a green background, small red squares, and a dark blue line, is mounted on a warm neutral plaster wall with a subtle dark walnut wood panel accent, in a midcentury-influenced collector's loft. The room features exposed polished concrete structural elements and floor, with clean architectural lines and spatial depth. The camera is positioned at human height (140 cm) within a concrete architectural recess, capturing a 3/4 oblique view with strong linear perspective leading towards a far wall, creating a single clean vanishing direction. The artwork is slightly off-center, framed by the recess and side concrete planes, creating depth and intimacy. A low-slung, iconic midcentury design chair (e.g., a Børge Mogensen Spanish Chair in aged, dark brown leather) is subtly placed in the foreground or midground, partially visible, supporting the artwork's dominance without competing. The space is bathed in soft, filtered warm daylight from large industrial-style windows, creating gentle shadows and a subtle burgundy-toned spatial mood, highlighting the concrete texture. The artwork must remain the protagonist, presented as a physical canvas with visible thickness, and its exact colors and proportions preserved.
